# Shortlist

> Self-hosted tool that gives every user on a Plex server a private, nightly-refreshed
> "Picked for You" collection row built from their own watch history, using per-user label
> restrictions (PMS >= 1.43.2) for privacy and an optional LLM for curation.

## Layout
- shortlist/engine/: pure pipeline library (history -> TMDB candidates -> rank -> curate ->
  deliver -> privacy sync). Never imports shortlist/server.
- shortlist/server/: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy/Alembic + APScheduler + SSE adapter over the engine;
  the built-in scheduler runs the engine nightly (no external cron).
- web/: React 18 + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui SPA.
- tests/: pytest (unit/integration, no network), tests/fakes/fake_plex.py stub server,
  recorded fixtures in tests/fixtures/.

## Key invariants
- Share-filter writes are read-modify-write merges; conditions Shortlist didn't add are
  byte-preserved. Snapshots precede first mutation; uninstall restores them.
- Only collections labeled shortlist_* are ever modified or deleted (Kometa coexistence).
- The owner is never restricted; managed-user restriction profiles are never modified.
- Rows are made private by leak-safe write ordering: delivered UNPROMOTED, each account's share
  filter gets `label!=shortlist_<slug>` for every other row, and only THEN are rows promoted — so a
  row is never visible before the exclusion that hides it exists. (The old automatic Privacy Check
  write-gate was removed 2026-07-16; the ordering is the guarantee.) Requires PMS >= 1.43.2.10687.
- Plex title-cases labels; filters must use the stored form.
- LLM output is validated against the owned-candidate set (no hallucinated titles).

## Docs
- docs/getting-started.md, docs/guides.md, docs/reference.md, docs/faq.md
- Design/architecture: .claude/docs/shortlist-design.md, .claude/docs/shortlist-architecture.md
